response.justification: Strong, versatile engineer with genuine large-scale distributed-systems experience (low-latency micro-services handling millions of requests/second) and clear mentoring/leadership signals. However, the role hinges on a hard minimum of 8 years of C++, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (~6 months) plus a bachelor thesis. The candidate is overwhelmingly a web/TypeScript/DLang generalist, with no cloud compute platform or production cloud architecture experience. Core C++ and compute-platform qualifications are not met.
strengths: Real distributed-systems depth: low-latency micro-services at millions of requests/second
Demonstrated leadership/mentoring: staff roles, OSS founder, workshops and dojos
Broad full-stack and infrastructure exposure including Docker, CI/CD, Kubernetes
concerns: Far short of the 8-year C++ minimum (only ~6 months of C/C++ work)
No cloud compute platform or production cloud architecture experience
No formal technical-lead title or large-ambiguous-project ownership evidence
